title: Specialist Training
description: A DeepSeek-V4 post-training pattern that sharpens separate capability areas before they are merged back into the final stack.
openingSummary: One general post-training pass can blur domain-specific strengths. Specialist training keeps certain domains or tasks sharper by letting them mature with more focused data and objectives before the final system is assembled.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Specialist training is the part of the V4 story where high-value domains are trained with more targeted supervision rather than only with one blended generic pass.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: It aims to preserve strong performance in demanding subdomains such as reasoning-heavy or verifiable tasks.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Data is organized around specialist capability pockets, those specialists are improved, and the results are folded back into the broader stack.

comparedToNearbyRegimes.title: Compared To Nearby Regimes
comparedToNearbyRegimes.body: Unlike one uniform post-training pass, specialist training accepts that not every domain should be shaped with identical data pressure.

limitationsAndFailureModes.title: Limitations And Failure Modes
limitationsAndFailureModes.body: Specialization can fragment the training story if the merge step is weak or if the chosen domains do not match real demand.

trainingFlow.title: Specialist Training flow
trainingFlow.alt: A flow from domain pockets to specialists to a merged model stack.
trainingFlow.caption: Specialist training raises some capability bands with focused supervision before merge.
